entries.subtitle: Turn any element into a flex container
entries.descLong: Setting display: flex on an element makes it a flex container. Its direct children become flex items. Use inline-flex to keep the container itself inline.
tips: Only **direct children** become flex items — grandchildren are unaffected
`display: inline-flex` makes the container itself inline while children are still flex items
Flexbox is one-dimensional — use CSS Grid for two-dimensional layouts
entries.mistake: Applying flex properties (justify-content, align-items) to the child instead of the container. These properties go on the parent.

entries.subtitle: Control whether items flow in a row or column
entries.descLong: flex-direction sets the main axis. row (default) flows items left to right. column flows top to bottom. The cross axis is always perpendicular to the main axis.
tips: Changing direction also swaps what `justify-content` and `align-items` affect
`column` direction makes height the main axis — items stack vertically
Use `row-reverse` for RTL layouts or to visually flip order without changing DOM order
entries.mistake: Forgetting that justify-content controls the MAIN axis and align-items controls the CROSS axis. When direction is column, justify-content controls vertical alignment.

entries.desc: Align items along the main axis.
entries.subtitle: Distribute space along the main axis
entries.signature: justify-content: flex-start | center | flex-end | space-between | space-around | space-evenly
entries.descLong: justify-content controls spacing along the main axis (horizontal in row, vertical in column). space-between puts space between items; space-evenly distributes space including edges.
tips: `space-between` — no space at edges; `space-around` — half space at edges; `space-evenly` — equal space everywhere
Only has effect if items don't fill the container — if they do, there's no space to distribute
In a column container, justify-content controls **vertical** alignment
entries.mistake: Using justify-content: center to center a single item vertically. In a row container, justify-content is horizontal. Use align-items: center for vertical centering.

entries.desc: Align items along the cross axis.
entries.subtitle: Control cross-axis alignment of flex items
entries.signature: align-items: stretch | center | flex-start | flex-end | baseline
entries.descLong: align-items controls alignment on the cross axis (vertical in row direction). Default is stretch — items fill the container height. Use center to vertically center items.
tips: Default is `stretch` — items grow to fill container height
`baseline` aligns items by their text baseline — useful for mixed font sizes
Use `align-self` on individual children to override the container's `align-items`
entries.mistake: Trying to vertically center with just justify-content. You need BOTH justify-content: center AND align-items: center for true centering.

entries.desc: Allow an item to grow to fill available space.
entries.subtitle: Proportionally expand items to fill remaining space
entries.descLong: flex-grow controls how much an item expands to fill available space. 0 means no growing. 1 means share space equally. 2 means take twice as much as items with flex-grow: 1.
tips: `flex-grow: 1` on a child makes it fill all remaining space
Ratios matter: two items with `flex-grow: 1` split space equally; one with `2` and one with `1` split 2:1
Use the `flex` shorthand: `flex: 1` sets `flex-grow: 1, flex-shrink: 1, flex-basis: 0`
entries.mistake: Setting flex-grow on the container instead of the child. flex-grow is a child property.

entries.desc: Set grow, shrink, and basis in one property.
entries.subtitle: The flex shorthand: grow / shrink / basis
entries.descLong: The flex shorthand sets flex-grow, flex-shrink, and flex-basis together. flex: 1 is the most common value — means grow: 1, shrink: 1, basis: 0% (divide space equally).
tips: `flex: 1` = `flex: 1 1 0%` — items start from 0 and divide space equally
`flex: auto` = `flex: 1 1 auto` — items start from natural size then distribute remaining space
`flex: none` = `flex: 0 0 auto` — item is rigid, doesn't grow or shrink
Prefer the `flex` shorthand over setting grow/shrink/basis individually
entries.mistake: Using flex: 1 and expecting items to be equal width when they have different content. Use flex: 1 1 0% or flex: 1 to ensure equal distribution from zero.
